summary: Lead accepts M5-WEB-WINDOWS-DISTRIBUTABLE-001 after release_engineer R2 REVIEW, independent security_reviewer ACCEPT, independent qa_reviewer ACCEPT, and lead validation gates. The branch adds a narrow MONSOON_SMOKE_WEB packaged-web evidence entry while preserving normal desktop shell and host-hash priority, plus a packaged M5 smoke script. The accepted boundary is local/smoke-only distributable evidence: M5 packaged launch, command preview/confirm, client-session save/load checkpoint, Electron security canaries, and host hash determinism. Import/export button count 0 remains an explicit caveat because no desktop file import/export IPC exists in this slice; future file import/export requires a separate reviewed bridge.

tests_run.result: VALID task file before lead acceptance update.

tests_run.command: lead gates rerun before security/QA route
tests_run.result: pnpm build:web, pnpm desktop:security-check, pnpm desktop:package, node apps/desktop/scripts/package-smoke.mjs, pnpm test:e2e, Electron host hash canary, desktop format/lint/typecheck, and git diff --check passed.
Both reviewers recorded material gates passing and accepted the current package-smoke/import-export caveat.

decisions: Accept task for PR/CI/main integration; do not mark CLOSED until PR CI passes, branch enters main, and local main is synchronized.
Accept MONSOON_SMOKE_WEB as a local evidence switch only. It does not change normal desktop shell behavior and does not override MONSOON_HOST_HASH_CANARY.

Accept import/export button count 0 as explicit caveat at current product boundary: desktop exposes no file import/export IPC, and package-smoke proves client-session save/load checkpoint without renderer filesystem access.
Preserve local/smoke-only scope. This task does not make a release, signing, store, telemetry, accounts, public launch, branding, or commercial decision.
Future desktop file import/export must be separately designed and reviewed with allowlisted dialogs, path constraints, schema validation, malformed-input tests, and security evidence.

risks.risk: Current ACCEPT covers client-session save/load checkpoint only, not future desktop file import/export UX or file-system adapter behavior.
A future product decision to launch M5 web UI by default in desktop requires a separate task; current normal mode remains security shell.

requested_action: Commit lead acceptance, push branch, open PR, wait for CI, merge into main, then close M5-WEB-WINDOWS-DISTRIBUTABLE-001 and continue to the next READY task.
